nodes.context: Russian state-funded private military company controlled by Yevgeny Prigozhin until 2023, then by Pavel Prigozhin. Operates globally, involved in conflicts in Ukraine, Syria, Libya, Central African Republic, Mali, and others.
The military forces of Russia. Wagner Group has used its infrastructure and has been used as a proxy by the Russian government.
Pro-Russian separatist groups in the Donbas region of Ukraine. Wagner helped them from 2014 to 2015.
Far-right extremist or neo-Nazi unit within the Wagner Group, fighting in eastern Ukraine.
Fighting arm of the Russian Imperial Movement, which ran a paramilitary training program that Rusich founders graduated from.
Military of Syria. Wagner supported regimes friendly with Russia, including in Syria.
Iranian military force. Listed as an ally of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Military of the Central African Republic. Wagner supported the regime there.
Recruited imprisoned UPC rebels in the Central African Republic to fight for Wagner. Listed as an ally in the infobox.
Listed as an ally of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Wagner supported regimes friendly with Russia, including in Libya.
Sudanese paramilitary force. Listed as an ally of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Military of Mozambique. Wagner deployed contractors there between 2017 and 2019.
Military of Mali. Wagner supported the regime there.
Listed as an ally of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Military of Ukraine. Wagner fought against them during the Russian invasion.
Syrian jihadist group. Listed as an opponent of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Syrian rebel group. Listed as an opponent of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Syrian rebel group. Listed as an opponent of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Kurdish-led alliance in Syria. Listed as an opponent of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Jihadist group. Listed as an opponent of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Syrian jihadist group. Listed as an opponent of Wagner Group in the infobox (2014–2017).
Central African rebel coalition. Listed as an opponent of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Forces opposing the Libyan National Army. Listed as an opponent of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Mozambican jihadist group. Listed as an opponent of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Listed as an opponent of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Listed as an opponent of Wagner Group in the infobox.
Russian state-funded private military company controlled by Yevgeny Prigozhin until 2023, operating in multiple countries.
Serb paramilitary unit active during the Bosnian War.
Serbian special police unit active during the Kosovo War.
Jihadist militant group that controlled territory in Syria and Iraq.
Military forces of the Russian Federation.
Russian military intelligence agency.
Russian national security service.
Russian state-funded private military company controlled by Yevgeny Prigozhin until 2023, active in Ukraine, Syria, Africa.
Russian troll factory used by Prigozhin to smear opposition and exploit grievances.
Russian Ministry of Defence structure formed in November 2023 to absorb Wagner's personnel and activities in Africa.
Russian state-funded private military company controlled by Yevgeny Prigozhin until 2023, involved in conflicts in Ukraine, Syria, Libya, CAR, Mali, and other countries.
Al-Qaeda affiliate in the Sahel region, active in Mali.
Pro-Maduro paramilitary groups in Venezuela.
Russian state-funded private military company controlled by Yevgeny Prigozhin until 2023, involved in conflicts in Ukraine, Syria, Libya, Central African Republic, and Mali.
Far-right ultranationalist organization linked to founding members of Wagner Group.
Wagner sub-group founded by self-proclaimed neo-Nazi Alexey Milchakov, open about its far-right ideology.
Chechen units that former Wagner fighters have joined, still wearing Wagner patches.

edges.evidence_quote: The Wagner Group has used infrastructure of the Russian Armed Forces.
edges.context: Wagner used Russian Armed Forces infrastructure.

edges.evidence_quote: The group emerged during the war in Donbas, where it helped Russian separatist forces in Ukraine from 2014 to 2015.
edges.context: Wagner helped Russian separatist forces in Ukraine from 2014 to 2015.

edges.evidence_quote: The Wagner Group includes a contingent known as Rusich, or Task Force Rusich
edges.context: Rusich is a unit within the Wagner Group.

edges.evidence_quote: The group was founded by Alexey Milchakov and Yan Petrovsky, both of them neo-Nazis, in the summer of 2014, after graduating from a paramilitary training program run by the Russian Imperial Legion
edges.context: Rusich founders graduated from a paramilitary training program run by the Russian Imperial Legion.

edges.evidence_quote: Wagner is believed to have a Serb unit, which was, until at least April 2016, under the command of Davor Savičić, a Bosnian Serb who was a member of the Serb Volunteer Guard (also known as Arkan's Tigers) during the Bosnian War
edges.context: A Wagner Serb unit was commanded by a former member of the Serb Volunteer Guard.

edges.evidence_quote: Wagner is believed to have a Serb unit, which was, until at least April 2016, under the command of Davor Savičić, a Bosnian Serb who was a member of the Serb Volunteer Guard (also known as Arkan's Tigers) during the Bosnian War and the Special Operations Unit (JSO) during the Kosovo War.
edges.context: A Wagner Serb unit was commanded by a former member of the Special Operations Unit.

edges.evidence_quote: He was also reported to had been involved in the first offensive to capture Palmyra from the Islamic State (ISIL) in early 2016.
edges.context: Wagner fought against ISIL in Syria.

edges.evidence_quote: The Wagner Group has used infrastructure of the Russian Armed Forces.
edges.context: Wagner used Russian Armed Forces infrastructure.

edges.evidence_quote: According to a Russia investigative media Russkiy Kriminal, the military command of 'Wagner' is held directly by the GRU
edges.context: Wagner's military command is held by the GRU.

edges.evidence_quote: Meanwhile, Russia's national security service, FSB, said it had filed criminal charges against Prigozhin and moved to arrest him.
edges.context: FSB filed charges against Wagner's leader during the rebellion.

edges.evidence_quote: He would use another arm of his business empire, a troll factory called the Internet Research Agency, to smear domestic opposition, popularize the client and further exploit grievances against the West.
edges.context: Prigozhin used the Internet Research Agency as part of his business empire to support Wagner's operations.

edges.evidence_quote: By the following year, the Wagner Group in Africa was merged into the 'Africa Corps'.
edges.context: Wagner Group was merged into the Africa Corps.

edges.evidence_quote: On 21 November, at least seven Wagner soldiers were killed by al-Qaeda affiliate JNIM in an ambush on a convoy in the Mopti region.
edges.context: JNIM ambushed and killed Wagner soldiers in Mali.

edges.evidence_quote: They assisted in the training of the Venezuelan National Militia and the pro-Maduro Colectivos paramilitaries in 2018.
edges.context: Wagner Group trained Colectivos paramilitaries in Venezuela.

edges.evidence_quote: Some founding members of Wagner belong to the far-right ultranationalist Russian Imperial Movement.
edges.context: Founding members of Wagner are members of the Russian Imperial Movement.

edges.evidence_quote: A Wagner sub-group, "Rusich", was founded by self-proclaimed neo-Nazi Alexey Milchakov and is open about its far-right ideology.
edges.context: Rusich is a sub-group of Wagner Group.

edges.evidence_quote: Other reports indicate that former Wagner fighters have joined Chechen Akhmat units, whilst still wearing Wagner patches.
edges.context: Former Wagner fighters have joined Akhmat units.
